exercises.name: Barbell Back Squat
exercises.setupNotes: Longer femurs require 30-35° torso lean. Bar on upper traps (NOT lower back). Drive through mid-foot. Keep vertical shin angle to reduce knee shear. Maintain neutral spine (L4-L5 protection).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 6-12 reps hypertrophy. Noa: L4-L5 risk if torso angle >35°. Boris: Top set + 2 back-off sets.

exercises.name: Front Squat
exercises.setupNotes: More upright torso (20-25°) reduces L4-L5 shear. Bar on front delts. Longer femurs benefit from heeled shoes. Elbows high, core braced.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-15 reps, quad-dominant. Noa: Safer for lower back vs back squat. Boris: Moderate weight, focus form.

exercises.name: Goblet Squat
exercises.setupNotes: Hold DB at chest, forces upright torso. Excellent for taller lifters. Elbow inside knees at bottom. Minimal spinal load.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps, great warm-up. Noa: Very safe for L4-L5. Boris: Pump work, slow tempo.

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.setupNotes: Rear foot elevated split squat.
exercises.committeeNotes: Quad and glute focus.

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.setupNotes: Feet high on platform (reduces hip flexion strain). Lower back flat against pad. Don't round lower back at bottom (L4-L5 safety).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-20 reps, high volume. Noa: Safe quad/glute builder if ROM controlled. Boris: Pyramids, partial reps at top.

exercises.name: Hack Squat
exercises.setupNotes: Fixed angle reduces torque variability. Shoulders against pads. Knees track over toes. Vertical shin reduces patellar stress.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-15 reps, quad emphasis. Noa: Lower back supported. Boris: Great for intensity techniques.

exercises.name: Safety Bar Squat
exercises.setupNotes: Bar weight forward shifts torso more upright. Reduces shoulder/elbow strain (medial epicondylitis safety). Maintains L4-L5 neutrality better than back squat.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 6-10 reps. Noa: Best squat variant for elbow/shoulder injuries. Boris: Build to heavy singles.

exercises.name: Box Squat
exercises.setupNotes: Box height at parallel or slightly above. Sit back aggressively (longer femurs). Pause on box, explode up. Teaches posterior chain.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 3-6 reps, power focus. Noa: Controlled depth protects L4-L5. Boris: Speed work, 50-70% 1RM.

exercises.name: Pause Squat
exercises.setupNotes: 2-3 second pause at bottom. Longer femurs = more quad demand. Maintain brace, don't relax at bottom (L4-L5). Builds bottom strength.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 4-8 reps, strength phase. Noa: Great for teaching depth control. Boris: 70-85% 1RM pauses.

exercises.name: Sumo Squat
exercises.setupNotes: Wide stance reduces forward lean. Toes out 30-45°. Knees track over toes. Reduces hip flexion demand (beneficial for taller lifters).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-12 reps, glute/adductor emphasis. Noa: Alternative if conventional hurts. Boris: Moderate weight, volume.

exercises.name: Conventional Deadlift
exercises.setupNotes: Longer femurs = higher hip start position. Hinge at hips, NOT round back. Neutral spine critical (L4-L5). Bar over mid-foot. Pull slack out first.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 3-8 reps. Noa: HIGH L4-L5 RISK - monitor closely. Boris: Build singles, limit volume.

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift (RDL) - BB
exercises.setupNotes: Hip hinge pattern. Longer torso = greater hamstring stretch. Keep bar close to shins. Slight knee bend. NEVER round lower back (L4-L5 priority).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-12 reps, hamstring hypertrophy. Noa: Moderate L4-L5 risk, watch form. Boris: Controlled tempo.

exercises.name: Trap Bar Deadlift
exercises.setupNotes: Neutral grip (elbow-safe). More upright torso vs conventional. Reduced L4-L5 shear. Handles allow longer range for taller lifters.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 5-10 reps, safer alternative. Noa: MUCH safer for L4-L5 and elbows. Boris: Great for high-rep work.

exercises.name: Sumo Deadlift
exercises.setupNotes: Wide stance reduces forward lean (L4-L5 benefit). Hips closer to bar. Toes out. Activates adductors. Shorter ROM for tall lifters.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 3-8 reps. Noa: Better L4-L5 loading vs conventional. Boris: Test max variation.

exercises.name: Single-Leg RDL
exercises.setupNotes: Longer levers = greater balance challenge. DB in opposite hand. Slight standing knee bend. Hinge from hip, NOT spine rotation.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps each leg. Noa: Minimal spinal load, safe unilateral. Boris: Stability work.

exercises.name: Deficit Deadlift
exercises.setupNotes: Stand on 1-2" platform. Increased ROM = greater posterior chain demand. CRITICAL: Maintain neutral spine (L4-L5 risk at deeper depth).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 3-6 reps, strength work. Noa: HIGH RISK - only with perfect form. Boris: Overload stretch, careful.

exercises.name: Rack Pull
exercises.setupNotes: Bar at knee height. Reduced ROM = heavier load. Targets upper back/traps. Less L4-L5 stress than conventional.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 3-8 reps, overload. Noa: Safer partial ROM for back. Boris: Supramax loading.

exercises.name: Stiff-Leg Deadlift
exercises.setupNotes: Legs almost straight (slight knee unlock). Greater hamstring stretch. Bar stays close. CRITICAL: Neutral spine always.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-12 reps, hamstring focus. Noa: Moderate L4-L5 risk. Boris: Light-moderate weight.

exercises.name: Barbell Flat Bench Press
exercises.setupNotes: Longer arms = longer ROM (strength disadvantage). Arch back, retract scapulae. Bar to lower chest. Elbows 45° (NOT 90° - elbow safety).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 6-12 reps. Noa: 45° elbow angle protects medial epicondyle. Boris: Comp lift, build technique.

exercises.name: Incline Barbell Bench Press
exercises.setupNotes: 30° bench angle. Upper chest emphasis. Bar to upper chest/clavicle area. Same 45° elbow angle for elbow safety.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-12 reps, clavicular pec. Noa: Elbow-safe pressing angle. Boris: Moderate weight, volume.

exercises.name: Decline Barbell Bench Press
exercises.setupNotes: 15-30° decline. Lower chest emphasis. Shorter ROM vs flat (strength advantage). Bar to lower sternum.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 6-10 reps, lower pec. Noa: Reduced shoulder stress. Boris: Heavy pressing variation.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Flat Bench Press
exercises.setupNotes: Greater ROM than barbell (stretch advantage). Natural arc path. Elbows 45°. Neutral wrist (elbow safety).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-15 reps, hypertrophy. Noa: Safer for elbows than barbell. Boris: Drop sets, volume work.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.setupNotes: 30-45° angle. DBs allow greater stretch. Arc toward midline at top. Shoulder-friendly path.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps, upper chest. Noa: Excellent for elbow health. Boris: Pyramids, slow eccentric.

exercises.name: Cable Chest Fly
exercises.setupNotes: Longer arms = greater stretch. Handles at chest height. Slight elbow bend (NOT straight - elbow protection). Squeeze pecs at center.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps, isolation. Noa: Very elbow-safe, constant tension. Boris: Pump work, drop sets.

exercises.name: Machine Chest Press
exercises.setupNotes: Adjust seat so handles at mid-chest. Fixed path reduces stabilizer demand. Good for high-rep work.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-20 reps. Noa: Very safe, supported. Boris: Finisher, pump sets.

exercises.name: Close-Grip Bench Press
exercises.setupNotes: Hands shoulder-width. Tricep emphasis. Elbows close to body (elbow safety). Bar to lower chest.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-12 reps, triceps. Noa: Monitor elbow - may aggravate medial epicondylitis. Boris: Accessory for lockout.

exercises.name: Push-Ups
exercises.setupNotes: Hands slightly wider than shoulders. Elbows 45°. Torso rigid. Full ROM (chest to floor).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-30 reps, endurance. Noa: Safe pressing, scalable. Boris: Warm-up, finisher.

exercises.name: Barbell Row
exercises.setupNotes: Hip hinge position. Torso 30-45° angle. Pull to lower sternum. Maintain neutral spine (L4-L5). Supinated grip may stress elbow.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 6-12 reps, lat/rhomboid. Noa: L4-L5 risk if form breaks, elbow stress with supination. Boris: Build strength, moderate volume.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Row
exercises.setupNotes: Hand on bench, torso parallel. Pull DB to hip. Neutral grip (elbow-safe). Don't rotate torso. Control eccentric.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps each side. Noa: Excellent for elbow safety, unilateral. Boris: Moderate-heavy, strict form.

exercises.name: Chest-Supported Row
exercises.setupNotes: Incline bench removes L4-L5 loading. Chest supported. Pull handles to sides. Focus on scapular retraction.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps. Noa: SAFEST rowing - no L4-L5 stress. Boris: Volume work, pump.

exercises.name: Cable Row
exercises.setupNotes: Seated, chest up. Pull handle to sternum. Elbows back. Neutral spine. Controlled return (constant tension).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps, mid-back. Noa: L4-L5 safe if upright. Boris: Drop sets, time under tension.

exercises.name: T-Bar Row
exercises.setupNotes: Hip hinge, chest up. Pull bar to sternum. Neutral grip (elbow-safe). Similar L4-L5 demands to barbell row.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-12 reps. Noa: Moderate L4-L5 risk, better elbow position. Boris: Heavy rowing variation.

exercises.name: Seal Row
exercises.setupNotes: Prone on bench. Bar underneath. Pull to bench. Removes L4-L5 entirely. Pure lat/rhomboid work.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-15 reps. Noa: SAFEST heavy rowing - zero spinal load. Boris: Strength work without fatigue.

exercises.name: Pendlay Row
exercises.setupNotes: Bar starts from floor each rep. Explosive pull. Torso near parallel. Reset each rep. High L4-L5 demand.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 5-8 reps, power. Noa: HIGH L4-L5 RISK - only if healthy. Boris: Strength/power phase.

exercises.name: Inverted Row
exercises.setupNotes: Under bar at waist height. Body rigid. Pull chest to bar. Elbows back. Scalable with bar height.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-20 reps. Noa: Very safe, no equipment. Boris: Warm-up, endurance.

exercises.name: Standing Barbell Overhead Press
exercises.setupNotes: Taller height = longer bar path. Bar starts at clavicle. Press straight up. Core braced (L4-L5 stability). Elbows forward at start.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 5-10 reps, strength. Noa: Shoulder/elbow stress - monitor. Boris: Top set + back-offs.

exercises.name: Seated Dumbbell Overhead Press
exercises.setupNotes: Seated removes leg drive. DBs allow natural arc. Press up and slightly together. Back supported (L4-L5 safe).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-12 reps. Noa: Safer than standing, supported spine. Boris: Hypertrophy work.

exercises.name: Arnold Press
exercises.setupNotes: Start palms facing you. Rotate while pressing. Hits all three delt heads. Controlled rotation (elbow safety).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps, complete delt. Noa: Monitor elbow during rotation. Boris: Moderate weight, pump.

exercises.name: Machine Shoulder Press
exercises.setupNotes: Adjust seat height. Handles start at shoulder level. Fixed path. Good for high-rep work.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-20 reps. Noa: Very safe, supported. Boris: Finisher, metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Landmine Press
exercises.setupNotes: Arc path easier on shoulders. One-arm or two-arm. Stand or half-kneel. Reduced shoulder stress vs vertical press.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps. Noa: Shoulder-friendly angle. Boris: Great accessory.

exercises.name: Pike Push-Up
exercises.setupNotes: Hips high, inverted V position. Head toward floor. Mimics overhead press pattern. Scalable bodyweight work.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-20 reps. Noa: Safe pressing pattern. Boris: Warm-up, finisher.

exercises.name: Pull-Up
exercises.setupNotes: Pronated grip, shoulder-width+. Full ROM (chest to bar). Scapulae depressed. Longer arms = greater challenge.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 5-15 reps, lat hypertrophy. Noa: Elbow-safe with pronated grip. Boris: Weight added if >12 reps.

exercises.name: Chin-Up
exercises.setupNotes: Supinated grip, shoulder-width. Bicep emphasis. Full ROM. Monitor elbow (supination stress on medial epicondyle).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 6-12 reps, bicep/lat. Noa: May aggravate elbow injury. Boris: Build to weighted.

exercises.name: Neutral Grip Pull-Up
exercises.setupNotes: Palms facing each other. Most elbow-friendly grip. Balanced lat/bicep. Excellent for injury prevention.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 6-15 reps. Noa: BEST grip for elbow safety. Boris: Primary variation for high volume.

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown
exercises.setupNotes: Wide grip, pronated. Pull bar to upper chest. Slight lean back. Squeeze lats. Scalable resistance.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps, lat width. Noa: Safe alternative to pull-ups. Boris: Drop sets, volume.

exercises.name: Close-Grip Lat Pulldown
exercises.setupNotes: Narrow V-bar or close handles. Pull to sternum. Lat thickness emphasis. Elbow-friendly.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps, mid-back. Noa: Very safe grip. Boris: Pump work.

exercises.name: Straight-Arm Pulldown
exercises.setupNotes: Arms nearly straight. Pull down in arc to hips. Lat isolation. Slight elbow bend (safety).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps, isolation. Noa: Safe lat work. Boris: Pre-exhaust or finisher.

exercises.name: Assisted Pull-Up
exercises.setupNotes: Machine counterbalances bodyweight. Build toward unassisted. Maintain full ROM and form.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-15 reps. Noa: Good for building strength safely. Boris: Progress to bodyweight.

exercises.name: Lying Leg Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Prone on bench. Pad at ankles. Curl heels to glutes. Hip stability critical. Don't arch lower back (L4-L5).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-20 reps, hamstring isolation. Noa: Safe, no spinal load. Boris: Drop sets, pump.

exercises.name: Seated Leg Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Hip flexed position. Greater hamstring stretch. Pad behind calves. Control eccentric.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps. Noa: Very safe, supported. Boris: Pump work.

exercises.name: Nordic Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Kneeling, ankles anchored. Lower torso slowly (eccentric). VERY challenging. Build tolerance gradually.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 3-8 reps, strength. Noa: Injury prevention gold standard. Boris: Negatives if can't do full.

exercises.name: Leg Extension
exercises.setupNotes: Pad at shins. Extend fully. Quad isolation. Avoid if knee pain.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps, quad isolation. Noa: Can stress patella - monitor knees. Boris: Pre-exhaust or finisher.

exercises.name: Standing Calf Raise
exercises.setupNotes: Full ROM (deep stretch to full contraction). Toes on platform edge. Knee straight for gastrocnemius emphasis.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-30 reps, high volume. Noa: Safe isolation. Boris: Straight sets, pump.

exercises.name: Seated Calf Raise
exercises.setupNotes: Knee bent 90°. Targets soleus (deep calf). Full ROM.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-30 reps. Noa: Safe, seated. Boris: Superset with standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Supinated grip. Elbows at sides. Curl to shoulders. Controlled eccentric. Monitor elbow (medial epicondyle stress).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-15 reps. Noa: May aggravate elbow injury. Boris: Moderate weight, strict form.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Alternating or simultaneous. Can supinate during curl. Neutral at bottom is elbow-friendlier.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps. Noa: Safer than barbell for elbows. Boris: Volume work.

exercises.name: Hammer Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Neutral grip (palms facing). Targets brachioradialis. SAFEST curl for elbow health.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps. Noa: BEST curl for medial epicondylitis. Boris: Primary bicep work.

exercises.name: Cable Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Constant tension. Low pulley. Curl handle to shoulders. Elbows stable.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps, pump. Noa: Moderate elbow stress. Boris: Finisher, drop sets.

exercises.name: Tricep Pushdown
exercises.setupNotes: High pulley. Rope or bar. Elbows at sides. Press down fully. Don't let elbows flare.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps. Noa: Monitor elbow - moderate stress. Boris: Drop sets, pump.

exercises.name: Overhead Tricep Extension
exercises.setupNotes: DB overhead. Lower behind head. Keep elbows close. Full stretch. Shoulder mobility required.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps, long head. Noa: Elbow/shoulder stress - monitor. Boris: Moderate weight.

exercises.name: Skull Crusher
exercises.setupNotes: Lying flat. Lower bar to forehead/behind head. Elbows stay in. HIGH elbow stress.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-12 reps. Noa: HIGH elbow risk - avoid if injured. Boris: EZ-bar reduces strain.

exercises.name: Dips
exercises.setupNotes: Lean forward for chest emphasis. Vertical for triceps. Full ROM. Monitor shoulder/elbow.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 6-15 reps. Noa: High shoulder/elbow stress. Boris: Weight added if >12 reps.

exercises.name: Lateral Raise
exercises.setupNotes: DBs at sides. Raise laterally to shoulder height. Slight elbow bend. Lead with elbows. Controlled descent.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps, side delts. Noa: Safe isolation. Boris: High rep, pump work.

exercises.name: Face Pull
exercises.setupNotes: Rope at face height. Pull to forehead. External rotation. Rear delt/rotator cuff health.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-25 reps. Noa: ESSENTIAL for shoulder health. Boris: Every session.

exercises.name: Rear Delt Fly
exercises.setupNotes: Bent over or chest-supported. DBs to sides in arc. Squeeze rear delts. Maintain neutral spine.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps. Noa: Safe, posture-improving. Boris: High rep work.

exercises.name: Shrugs
exercises.setupNotes: DBs at sides. Elevate shoulders (NOT roll). Squeeze traps at top. Controlled descent.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps, trap mass. Noa: Safe isolation. Boris: Heavy weight, volume.

exercises.name: Plank
exercises.setupNotes: Forearms on ground. Body rigid. Neutral spine (L4-L5 protection). Hold time over reps.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 30-90 sec holds. Noa: CRITICAL for L4-L5 stability. Boris: Every session.

exercises.name: Ab Wheel Rollout
exercises.setupNotes: Roll forward maintaining flat back.
exercises.committeeNotes: Elite core tension.

exercises.name: Cable Crunch
exercises.setupNotes: High pulley, rope. Kneeling. Crunch down (flex spine). Abs do the work, not hip flexors.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-25 reps, ab mass. Noa: Safe weighted ab work. Boris: Progressive overload.

exercises.name: Russian Twist
exercises.setupNotes: Seated rotation side to side.
exercises.committeeNotes: Oblique focus.

exercises.name: Hanging Leg Raise
exercises.setupNotes: Hanging raise of legs to 90 degrees.
exercises.committeeNotes: Lower ab focus.

exercises.name: Farmer's Walk
exercises.setupNotes: Heavy DBs at sides. Walk with neutral spine. Core braced. Grip endurance + core stability.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 30-60 sec walks. Noa: Excellent functional strength. Boris: Heavy, short distance.

exercises.name: Sled Push
exercises.setupNotes: Lean forward, drive through legs. Minimal L4-L5 compression. Excellent conditioning.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 20-60 sec pushes. Noa: Very safe, no eccentric damage. Boris: Finisher or conditioning.

exercises.name: Sled Pull
exercises.setupNotes: Backward walk or row. Rope/strap. Posterior chain emphasis. Safe high-volume work.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 20-60 sec pulls. Noa: Safe, no eccentric. Boris: Conditioning between sets.

exercises.name: Battle Ropes
exercises.setupNotes: Athletic stance. Waves, slams, or circles. Full body conditioning. Core braced.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 20-40 sec rounds. Noa: Safe conditioning. Boris: Finisher or warm-up.

exercises.name: Box Jump
exercises.setupNotes: Explosive jump onto elevated surface.
exercises.committeeNotes: Plyometric power.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Slam
exercises.setupNotes: Ball overhead, slam to ground. Full body power. Pick up and repeat. Great for power endurance.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps. Noa: Safe explosive work. Boris: Conditioning or warm-up.

exercises.name: Broad Jump
exercises.setupNotes: Horizontal jump for distance. Arm swing for momentum. Land softly. Triple extension power.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 3-6 reps. Noa: Land correctly - knee safety. Boris: Power testing.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Swing
exercises.setupNotes: Hip hinge pattern. Explosive hip drive. KB to chest height. Posterior chain power. L4-L5 demands if form breaks.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-25 reps. Noa: Moderate L4-L5 risk. Boris: Power endurance.

exercises.name: Turkish Get-Up
exercises.setupNotes: Lying to standing with KB overhead. Complex movement. Shoulder stability + core control. Learn progressions first.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 3-5 reps each side. Noa: Shoulder/core strength. Boris: Skill work, light weight.

exercises.name: Glute Bridge
exercises.setupNotes: Lie on back, lift hips.
exercises.committeeNotes: Glute isolation.

exercises.name: Hip Thrust
exercises.setupNotes: Same as glute bridge but greater ROM. Bar across hips. Full hip extension. Glute activation.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-15 reps. Noa: Safe, glute-dominant. Boris: Build to heavy loads.

exercises.name: Wall Sit
exercises.setupNotes: Back against wall, knees at 90 degrees.
exercises.committeeNotes: Isometric quad endurance.

exercises.name: Step-Up
exercises.setupNotes: Step up onto elevated surface.
exercises.committeeNotes: Unilateral leg strength.

exercises.name: Reverse Lunge
exercises.setupNotes: Step backward into lunge. Front knee 90°. DBs at sides. Easier on knees than forward lunge.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps each leg. Noa: Knee-friendly lunge. Boris: Volume work.

exercises.name: Walking Lunge
exercises.setupNotes: Longer stride for taller lifters. Alternate legs. DBs at sides. Functional leg work.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-20 steps total. Noa: Safe unilateral. Boris: Conditioning element.

exercises.name: Sissy Squat
exercises.setupNotes: Lean back while squatting. Knees forward. Extreme quad stretch. Advanced movement.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-15 reps. Noa: High knee stress - careful. Boris: Quad finisher.

exercises.name: Good Morning
exercises.setupNotes: Bar on upper back. Hip hinge forward. Hamstring/glute/lower back. HIGH L4-L5 demand. Caution.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-12 reps. Noa: HIGH L4-L5 RISK - only if healthy. Boris: Accessory, light-moderate.

exercises.name: Preacher Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Arms on pad. Isolated bicep work. NO momentum. Can stress elbow in bottom stretch.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps. Noa: Elbow stress - avoid if injured. Boris: Strict form, peak contraction.

exercises.name: Concentration Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Seated, elbow against inner thigh. Isolated bicep. Slow tempo. Peak contraction focus.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps. Noa: Safe isolation. Boris: Finisher, pump.

exercises.name: Incline Curl
exercises.setupNotes: 45° bench. Arms hang down. Greater bicep stretch. Supinate at top.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps. Noa: Moderate elbow stress. Boris: Long head emphasis.

exercises.name: Tricep Kickback
exercises.setupNotes: Bent over. Elbow high. Extend DB back. Squeeze tricep. Light weight, strict form.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps. Noa: Safe, low elbow stress. Boris: Finisher, pump.

exercises.name: Diamond Push-Up
exercises.setupNotes: Hands together forming diamond. Tricep emphasis. Harder than standard push-ups.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-20 reps. Noa: Moderate elbow stress. Boris: Bodyweight tricep work.

exercises.name: Bench Dips
exercises.setupNotes: Hands on bench behind. Feet on ground or elevated. Lower and press. Shoulder/elbow stress if too deep.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-20 reps. Noa: Monitor shoulder/elbow depth. Boris: High rep work.

exercises.name: Upright Row
exercises.setupNotes: Pull bar to chin. Elbows high. Traps/side delts. Can impinge shoulder - use wide grip.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps. Noa: Shoulder impingement risk - careful. Boris: Moderate weight.

exercises.name: Front Raise
exercises.setupNotes: DBs at thighs. Raise forward to shoulder height. Front delt isolation. Alternate or simultaneous.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps. Noa: Safe isolation. Boris: Pump work.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.setupNotes: Low pulley. Cable crosses body. Raise laterally. Constant tension. One arm at a time.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-25 reps. Noa: Safe, shoulder-friendly. Boris: Drop sets.

exercises.name: Reverse Pec Deck
exercises.setupNotes: Chest against pad. Arms wide. Squeeze rear delts. Posterior delt isolation.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-25 reps. Noa: Safe, supported. Boris: High rep pump.

exercises.name: Adductor Machine
exercises.setupNotes: Legs on pads. Squeeze together. Inner thigh work. Safe isolation.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-25 reps. Noa: Safe, no knee stress. Boris: Accessory work.

exercises.name: Abductor Machine
exercises.setupNotes: Legs on pads. Push out. Outer hip/glute medius. Hip stability.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-25 reps. Noa: Hip health, injury prevention. Boris: Accessory work.

exercises.name: Donkey Calf Raise
exercises.setupNotes: Bent over, pad on lower back. Full calf stretch. High volume calf work.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 20-40 reps. Noa: Safe, no spinal load. Boris: Pump work.

exercises.name: Wrist Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Forearms on bench. Wrists over edge. Curl DBs up. Grip/forearm work.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-25 reps. Noa: Forearm health. Boris: Accessory, grip strength.

exercises.name: Dead Hang
exercises.setupNotes: Hang from bar with relaxed shoulders.
exercises.committeeNotes: Grip strength and spinal decompression.

exercises.name: Decline Dumbbell Press
exercises.setupNotes: Feet anchored, 15-20° decline. Lower DBs to lower pec line, elbows 45-60° from torso. Squeeze chest at top. Targets lower pec fibres and reduces anterior shoulder stress.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-15 reps, excellent lower chest isolation. Noa: Lower spinal load than decline BB press. Boris: 3x10-12, use as secondary chest movement.

exercises.name: Pec Deck Fly
exercises.setupNotes: Seat height: handles at mid-chest. Maintain slight elbow bend throughout. Focus on adduction, not pressing. Slow eccentric 3-4s for maximum stretch. Keep chest tall.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps, best chest isolation at long muscle length. Noa: Avoid over-stretching if shoulder impingement history. Boris: 3x15-20, finisher.

exercises.name: Cable Crossover
exercises.setupNotes: High pulleys. Step forward, slight torso lean. Arms arc downward meeting hands below sternum. Maintain tension at full contraction. Elbows soft, not locked.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-15 reps, constant tension advantage over DBs. Noa: Low shoulder stress when cued correctly. Boris: 3x12-15, post-press finisher.

exercises.name: Ring Push-Up
exercises.setupNotes: Rings at wrist height. Turn rings out at top (external rotation). Protract scapulae at top for serratus activation. Instability demands greater stabiliser recruitment.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-15 reps, high pec activation + stabiliser demand. Noa: Avoid if wrist pain; add rings slowly. Boris: 3x8-12, excellent bodyweight progression.

exercises.name: Weighted Push-Up
exercises.setupNotes: Partner places weight plate on upper back (not neck). Hands slightly wider than shoulder-width. Elbows 45° from torso. Full ROM — chest touches floor. Brace core throughout.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-15 reps, easily progressive via load. Noa: Core must stay rigid to protect lumbar. Boris: Load weekly, treat like bench press progression.

exercises.name: Smith Machine Bench Press
exercises.setupNotes: Position bench so bar descends to mid-chest. Slightly angled bar path vs free-weight. Allows aggressive overload with reduced stability demand. Arch and leg drive still apply.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 6-12 reps, good for beginners or rehabbing shoulder. Noa: Fixed bar path reduces rotator cuff demand. Boris: Useful for overload phases.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Pullover
exercises.setupNotes: Lie perpendicular on bench, hips low. Both hands grip one DB. Lower DB behind head in arc, elbows slightly bent. Stretch long head of pec and lats. Pull back to start.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-15 reps, hits pec-lat seam. Noa: High shoulder mobility demand, skip if impingement. Boris: 3x12, good chest finisher.

exercises.name: Decline Push-Up
exercises.setupNotes: Feet elevated on bench/box, hands on floor. Body forms diagonal line. Greater upper pec and anterior delt involvement vs flat push-up. Keep hips level throughout.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-20 reps, increases upper pec load. Noa: Very safe; scalable with box height. Boris: Great warm-up or finisher, add vest for progression.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Chest Fly
exercises.setupNotes: Flat bench. DBs start over chest, slight bend in elbows maintained throughout. Lower in wide arc to stretch pec, elbows at shoulder height. Drive up squeezing chest. Control eccentric.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-15 reps, excellent pec stretch. Noa: High shoulder stress at bottom; limit ROM if impingement. Boris: 3x12, post-press accessory.

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.setupNotes: Opposite arm/leg movement on back.
exercises.committeeNotes: Spinal stability.

exercises.name: Pallof Press
exercises.setupNotes: Stand sideways to cable at chest height. Hold handle at sternum, brace core. Press hands straight out, hold 2s, return. Anti-rotation demand increases with load. Feet shoulder-width.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-12 reps, top anti-rotation exercise. Noa: Extremely safe, no spinal flexion. Boris: 3x10/side, essential for lifters with rotation deficits.

exercises.name: Dragon Flag
exercises.setupNotes: Lift entire body in straight line from bench.
exercises.committeeNotes: Elite anti-extension.

exercises.name: Landmine Rotation
exercises.setupNotes: Hold bar end at chest height, arms extended. Rotate bar from hip to hip in arc, pivoting through core. Hips should counter-rotate slightly. Control deceleration phase.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15/side, rotational power + oblique strength. Noa: Moderate spinal load, safe when controlled. Boris: 3x10/side, great for athletes.

exercises.name: Hollow Body Hold
exercises.setupNotes: Lie on back, press lower back to floor, lift legs and arms.
exercises.committeeNotes: Foundational gymnastics core strength.

exercises.name: Side Plank
exercises.setupNotes: Elbow under shoulder, body in straight line. Brace core and glutes.
exercises.committeeNotes: Hold for time. Excellent for obliques.

exercises.name: Bicycle Crunch
exercises.setupNotes: Lie on back, hands behind head lightly. Rotate elbow toward opposite knee while extending other leg. Full rotation from thorax, not just shoulder reach. Controlled tempo.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-20/side, high oblique EMG. Noa: Controlled speed prevents neck strain. Boris: 3x15/side, excellent oblique accessory.

exercises.name: L-Sit
exercises.setupNotes: Support body with hands, lift legs parallel to floor.
exercises.committeeNotes: High core and hip flexor demand.

exercises.name: Landmine Anti-Rotation Press
exercises.setupNotes: Stand 90° to landmine, hold bar end at chest. Press out and rotate toward landmine simultaneously. Resist twisting with core. Excellent oblique and rotator cuff integration.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-12/side, rotational stability. Noa: Low compressive load on spine. Boris: 3x10/side, pairs well with landmine rotation.

exercises.name: Suitcase Carry
exercises.setupNotes: Hold heavy DB in one hand at side. Walk 20-40m maintaining upright posture without lateral lean. Anti-lateral flexion demand. Shoulder packed, core braced throughout.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 20-40m/side, functional strength. Noa: Safe, excellent for spinal stability asymmetry. Boris: 3 trips/side, heavy and slow.

exercises.name: EZ Bar Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Angled grip (15-30°) reduces supination, easier on wrists than BB curl. Elbows fixed at sides. Full extension at bottom, full supination at top. Squeeze biceps peak.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-15 reps, wrist-friendly bicep builder. Noa: Lower wrist stress than straight bar — great for those with wrist issues. Boris: 3x10, heavy and controlled.

exercises.name: EZ Bar Skullcrusher
exercises.setupNotes: Lie on bench. Lower EZ bar toward forehead/behind head, elbows tracking forward (not flaring). Long head of tricep maximally stretched. Press back to lockout. Wrists neutral.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-12 reps, excellent long-head tricep. Noa: Less elbow stress than straight bar. Boris: 3x10, pair with close-grip bench.

exercises.name: Cable Overhead Tricep Extension
exercises.setupNotes: Face away from low pulley. Lean forward slightly, elbows beside head. Extend forearms overhead, maintaining elbow position. Long head fully stretched at bottom. Constant tension.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps, long head tricep emphasis. Noa: Lower wrist stress vs dumbbells. Boris: 3x15, excellent finisher.

exercises.name: Reverse Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Overhand (pronated) grip. Curl bar up, wrists neutral. Targets brachialis and brachioradialis more than bicep. Elbows fixed at sides. Full extension at bottom.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps, thick forearm and brachialis development. Noa: Wrist stability required, use EZ bar if discomfort. Boris: 3x12, pairs with hammer curl.

exercises.name: Rope Tricep Pushdown
exercises.setupNotes: Rope attachment at high pulley. Pull down and flare rope ends at bottom for lateral head activation. Elbows fixed. Full extension with wrist rotation. Avoid forward lean.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps, lateral and medial tricep head. Noa: Very safe, no joint stress. Boris: 3x15-20, classic finisher.

exercises.name: Zottman Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Curl up with supinated grip (bicep concentric). At top, pronate to overhand grip. Lower slowly with pronated grip (brachioradialis eccentric). Two exercises in one movement.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-12 reps, hits all three elbow flexors. Noa: Moderate forearm stress on eccentric. Boris: 3x10, slow eccentric — 3-4s down.

exercises.name: Tate Press
exercises.setupNotes: Lie on bench. DBs start over chest pointing up. Flare elbows out and lower DB ends toward chest (medial head emphasis). Press back up without full lockout. Elbows stay high.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-15 reps, unique medial head stimulus. Noa: Low joint stress when elbows tracked correctly. Boris: 3x12, great tricep variety.

exercises.name: Machine Preacher Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Upper arm on pad, no cheating possible. Full ROM — complete extension to full flexion. Pause at peak contraction. Pad angle determines where peak tension occurs.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps, excellent for peak bicep development. Noa: Safe, fixed path prevents elbow drift. Boris: 3x12, go heavier than free-weight preacher.

exercises.name: Cable Hammer Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Rope or single handle at low pulley. Neutral (hammer) grip throughout. Constant tension advantage over DB hammer curl. Targets brachialis and brachioradialis.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-15 reps, brachialis thickness builder. Noa: Very safe, wrist-neutral position. Boris: 3x12-15, pairs with supinated curls.

exercises.name: Spider Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Lie prone on incline bench. Arms hang straight down. Curl up keeping elbows pointed at floor. Peak contraction when forearm is vertical. Maximises stretch at bottom.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-12 reps, high stretch + peak contraction. Noa: Low cheating potential, strict form. Boris: 3x10, excellent bicep peak builder.

exercises.name: JM Press
exercises.setupNotes: Hybrid between skullcrusher and close-grip bench. Lower bar toward chin keeping elbows in front. Forearms nearly parallel to floor at bottom. Good for powerlifters building tricep lockout.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 6-10 reps, mass builder for tricep. Noa: High elbow stress — warm up thoroughly. Boris: 3x8, heavy compound tricep work.

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift (RDL) - DB
exercises.setupNotes: DBs at hip crease. Hip hinge with soft knees. Maintain neutral spine, bar path close to legs. Feel hamstring stretch at shin level. Drive hips forward to return. Same cues as BB RDL.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps, greater ROM than BB variant. Noa: Wrist neutral, less lower back demand than BB. Boris: 3x10-12, ideal for unilateral progressions.

exercises.name: Weighted Pull-Up
exercises.setupNotes: Attach weight via dip belt or hold DB between feet. Initiate with scapular depression. Elbows drive down and back. Chin clears bar, full extension at bottom. Maintain upright torso.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 4-8 reps, strongest lat stimulus. Noa: Ensure shoulder mobility before heavy loading. Boris: 3-5x5 heavy, treat like an upper body deadlift.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Row
exercises.setupNotes: Lie prone on incline bench (30-45°). DBs hang straight. Retract scapulae and drive elbows up and back. Chest stays on pad — eliminates momentum. Excellent upper back isolation.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps, strict form advantage over dumbbell row. Noa: Very safe, no lower back involvement. Boris: 3x12, great for balanced back development.

exercises.name: Single-Arm Cable Row
exercises.setupNotes: Cable at chest height, single handle. Seated or standing. Allow thoracic rotation at full extension; retract scapula and row to hip. Unilateral work corrects L/R imbalances.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15/side, excellent unilateral lat and rhomboid work. Noa: Low spinal load. Boris: 3x12/side, rotate through full range.

exercises.name: Meadows Row
exercises.setupNotes: Landmine-style: stand perpendicular to end of barbell on floor. Single-arm row with overhand grip. Elbow drives high and outside torso, targeting upper/outer lat and teres major.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-12/side, unique lat + upper back stimulus. Noa: Moderate lower back demand — brace core. Boris: 3x10/side, often forgotten exercise.

exercises.name: Wide-Grip Lat Pulldown
exercises.setupNotes: Grip 1.5x shoulder-width overhand. Lean back 10-15°. Elbows drive down and slightly back. Bar to upper chest. Avoid excessive rear-lean or momentum. Slow eccentric 2-3s.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-15 reps, lat width emphasis. Noa: Avoid behind-the-neck variant — cervical spine risk. Boris: 3x10-12, classic pulldown.

exercises.name: Cable Pullover
exercises.setupNotes: High pulley, rope attachment. Stand facing cable, hinge at hip. Pull rope down and back in arc, elbows soft. Targets lat at long muscle length. Finish with arms beside hips.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-15 reps, excellent lat isolation at long length. Noa: Low spinal stress. Boris: 3x15, great lat pump finisher.

exercises.name: Barbell Shrug
exercises.setupNotes: Overhand or mixed grip. Elevate shoulders straight up — no rolling. Hold top 1-2s for trap squeeze. Full depression at bottom. Heavier loading possible than DB shrug.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-15 reps, upper trapezius thickness. Noa: No shoulder rolling — rotator cuff stress. Boris: 3x10 heavy, add straps for grip.

exercises.name: Kneeling Single-Arm Lat Pulldown
exercises.setupNotes: Kneel facing cable, single handle overhead. Pull elbow down to hip, allowing slight lat stretch and thoracic rotation. Unilateral; corrects pulldown strength imbalances.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15/side, unilateral lat emphasis. Noa: Kneeling eliminates lower back involvement. Boris: 3x12/side, pair with bilateral pulldown.

exercises.name: Seated Barbell Overhead Press
exercises.setupNotes: Seated removes leg drive — pure shoulder and tricep. Bar slightly in front of head, slight forward lean. Elbows 45-60° from torso. Press to lockout. Use lighter load than standing OHP.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-12 reps, strict shoulder overload. Noa: Lower lumbar load vs standing OHP. Boris: 3x10, excellent for shoulder hypertrophy block.

exercises.name: Z-Press
exercises.setupNotes: Seated on floor, legs straight out. Press barbell overhead. No back support — requires extreme core and hip flexor stability. Eliminates any leg assistance. Very humbling load.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 5-10 reps, pure overhead pressing strength. Noa: High mobility demand — needs hamstring flexibility. Boris: 3x8, excellent OHP movement quality check.

exercises.name: Cable Front Raise
exercises.setupNotes: Low pulley, cable between legs or beside hip. Raise arm to shoulder height, elbow soft. Constant tension advantage vs DB front raise. Internal rotation at top targets anterior delt.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-15 reps, anterior delt isolation. Noa: Low joint stress. Boris: 3x15, only add if pressing is insufficient for front delt.

exercises.name: Machine Lateral Raise
exercises.setupNotes: Adjust seat so arm pad contacts at elbow crease. Abduct arms to shoulder height. Machine provides constant resistance curve. Easier to drop set than cable or dumbbell version.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-25 reps, best for high-volume lateral delt work. Noa: Very safe, fixed path. Boris: 3-4x15-20, excellent for shoulder width.

exercises.name: Cable Upright Row
exercises.setupNotes: Low pulley, straight bar or rope. Pull elbows high and wide, bar to chin. Elbows MUST stay above wrists throughout. Wide grip reduces shoulder impingement risk vs narrow grip.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps, lateral delt + upper trap. Noa: Wide grip essential — narrow grip impingement risk. Boris: 3x12, moderate load.

exercises.name: Band Pull-Apart
exercises.setupNotes: Hold band at shoulder height with overhand grip. Pull band apart to chest, squeezing rear delts and rhomboids. Keep arms straight throughout. Excellent scapular health movement.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-25 reps, posterior shoulder health. Noa: Essential prehab for heavy pressers. Boris: 2-3x20 every session before pressing.

exercises.name: Pistol Squat
exercises.setupNotes: Single leg deep squat.
exercises.committeeNotes: Elite unilateral leg strength.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Press
exercises.setupNotes: One foot on platform, slightly off-center. Lower to 90° knee flexion, drive through heel. Other foot on ground for stability. Corrects bilateral strength deficits between legs.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15/side, unilateral quad focus. Noa: Safe, no axial loading. Boris: 3x12/side, excellent post-injury rehabilitation.

exercises.name: Belt Squat
exercises.setupNotes: Load attached at hips via belt, not axial spine loading. Upright torso. Squat through full ROM. Eliminates spinal compression — ideal for back pain recovery or high volume.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-15 reps, quad-dominant without spinal load. Noa: Best alternative when back cannot tolerate barbell. Boris: High-volume blocks, 4x15.

exercises.name: Leg Press Calf Raise
exercises.setupNotes: Feet at bottom of leg press platform, toes and balls of feet on edge. Extend ankles fully (plantarflex), slow eccentric into dorsiflexion for full stretch. Heavier loading than standing calf raise.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-25 reps, seated position hits soleus more. Noa: Very safe position. Boris: 4x20, high volume for calf hypertrophy.

exercises.name: Cable Pull-Through
exercises.setupNotes: Stand facing away from cable, rope between legs. Hip hinge movement: push hips back, feel hamstring stretch, drive hips forward. Teaches hip hinge pattern with lower back emphasis.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-15 reps, hip hinge teaching tool + glute/hamstring. Noa: Very safe, excellent for beginners learning deadlift pattern. Boris: 3x15, warm-up for deadlift day.

exercises.name: Landmine Squat
exercises.setupNotes: Hold bar end at chest, feet shoulder-width. Natural arc of bar encourages upright torso. Great for beginners or when barbell squat is contraindicated. Heels elevated option increases quad focus.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps, beginner-friendly compound squat. Noa: Low spinal load. Boris: 3x12, higher reps work well.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Clean
exercises.setupNotes: KB between feet. Hike back, hip snap drives KB up. Catch in rack position: elbow down, KB resting on forearm, wrist neutral. Avoid banging forearm by inserting hand early.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 5-8/side, explosive hip power. Noa: Wrist injury risk if rack catch not cued. Boris: 3x5/side, technique before load.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Press
exercises.setupNotes: From rack position, press KB overhead. Lat engagement throughout. Wrist stays straight (KB rests on forearm). Glutes and core tight. At top, bicep by ear, elbow fully locked.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 5-8/side, excellent shoulder stability. Noa: High rotator cuff demand — beneficial for shoulder health. Boris: 3x6/side, technique focus.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Snatch
exercises.setupNotes: Single arm. Hip hike, explosive hip snap, guide KB overhead in one movement. Insert hand at top to avoid wrist bang. High-skill movement — requires clean proficiency first.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 5-10/side, total body power and conditioning. Noa: High technique demand, learn with light KB. Boris: 3x5/side, test with 5min/100 rep challenge.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Row
exercises.setupNotes: Hinge at hip, neutral spine. KB hangs from shoulder. Initiate with shoulder blade retraction, then elbow drive. Handle grip allows natural wrist rotation through ROM.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-12/side, good lat + rhomboid. Noa: Lower back demand moderate — brace core. Boris: 3x10/side, great for home gym or travel.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.setupNotes: Hold KB at hip crease. Same hip hinge as BB RDL. KB naturally hangs between legs. Excellent for single-leg variation (KB in opposite hand). Full hamstring stretch at bottom.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps, hamstring and glute hinge. Noa: Low spinal load vs barbell. Boris: 3x12, good for beginners learning hip hinge.

exercises.name: Band Bicep Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Stand on band, handles in hands. Curl to shoulder height maintaining elbow position. Band provides increasing resistance through ROM — different stimulus to free weights.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-20 reps, home gym bicep option. Noa: Very safe, low joint stress. Boris: 3x15-20, excellent for travel or deload weeks.

exercises.name: Band Tricep Pushdown
exercises.setupNotes: Anchor band at head height. Push hands down to hips, elbows fixed at sides. Full extension at bottom. Band resistance peaks at lockout — different to cable. High-rep pump work.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 20-30 reps, excellent pump. Noa: Very safe for elbows. Boris: 3x25, finisher or home workout.

exercises.name: Band Hip Thrust
exercises.setupNotes: Band across hips anchored to heavy object. Shoulder on bench, drive hips to ceiling. Posterior pelvic tilt at top for glute squeeze. Knees track over toes throughout.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-25 reps, peak glute contraction stimulus. Noa: Very safe. Boris: 3x20, warm-up or accessory for heavier hip thrusts.

exercises.name: Band Lateral Walk
exercises.setupNotes: Band around ankles or just above knees. Slight squat stance, toe slightly out. Step laterally maintaining band tension. Targets hip abductors and glute medius. Walk 10 steps each direction.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-20 steps/side, glute medius activation. Noa: Essential knee health prehab for squatters. Boris: 2-3x15/side before lower body session.

exercises.name: Band Pull-Apart (Resistance Band)
exercises.setupNotes: Hold band overhand at shoulder height, shoulder-width apart. Extend arms and pull band apart to chest width, squeezing rear delts. Return slowly. Keep elbows soft throughout.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 20-25 reps, posterior chain shoulder health. Noa: Critical for heavy pressers — prevents AC joint issues. Boris: 3x20 every upper body session.

exercises.name: Smith Machine Squat
exercises.setupNotes: Feet slightly forward of bar for vertical shin. Fixed bar path requires adjustment vs free squat. Lower to parallel or below. Good for beginners building pattern or heavy singles.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-12 reps, controlled overload. Noa: Fixed path changes natural biomechanics — not a free squat replacement. Boris: Use for volume work, not primary strength.

exercises.name: Smith Machine Overhead Press
exercises.setupNotes: Seated or standing. Bar starts at upper chest. Press straight up on fixed path. Fixed bar eliminates rotator cuff stabilisation — allows overload focus. Slight forward lean if seated.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-12 reps, controlled shoulder overload. Noa: Fixed path reduces rotator cuff demand — consider as secondary press. Boris: 3x10, drop sets effective.

exercises.name: Smith Machine Romanian Deadlift
exercises.setupNotes: Fixed bar path allows focused hamstring stretch without bar drift. Feet slightly under bar. Hip hinge with neutral spine. Feel hamstring stretch before returning. Good for beginners.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps, hamstring isolation. Noa: Vertical bar path differs from free RDL. Boris: 3x12, moderate load.

exercises.name: Smith Machine Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.setupNotes: Rear foot elevated, front foot forward of bar. Fixed bar on upper traps. Drop rear knee toward floor. Front shin stays relatively vertical. Fixed path allows aggressive overloading.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-12/side, heavy unilateral loading. Noa: Easier to balance than free-weight version. Boris: 3x10/side, excellent for strength phase.

exercises.name: Power Clean
exercises.setupNotes: Start in deadlift position. Explode upward, shrug, and 'catch' the bar on shoulders in a partial squat.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: High threshold motor unit recruitment. Noa: Requires excellent wrist and shoulder rack mobility. Boris: Use for explosive power, low reps (1-5).

exercises.name: Barbell Snatch
exercises.setupNotes: Wide grip. Explode from floor to overhead in one fluid motion. Catch in a squat or power position.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: Total body coordination and power. Noa: High technical demand; shoulder stability is paramount. Boris: Focus on speed and technique over load.

exercises.name: Clean and Jerk
exercises.setupNotes: Clean the bar to shoulders, then drive it overhead with a split or power jerk. Full lockout required.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: Ultimate metabolic and power demand. Noa: Heavy impact on joints; ensure proper footwear. Boris: The king of Oly lifts for total strength.

exercises.name: Muscle-Up (Rings)
exercises.setupNotes: False grip on rings. Pull high, lean forward through the 'transition', and press out into a dip.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: Incredible upper body pulling and pushing integration. Noa: High stress on the medial elbow and rotator cuff. Boris: Advanced skill move.

exercises.name: Handstand Push-Up
exercises.setupNotes: Kick up against a wall. Lower head to floor, then press back to full lockout. Keep core tight.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: Bodyweight equivalent to a heavy OHP. Noa: Maintain neutral neck; avoid 'craning' to look at floor. Boris: Progress to deficits for more ROM.

exercises.name: Burpee (Standard)
exercises.setupNotes: Drop to plank, touch chest to floor, jump feet back in, and jump vertically with hands overhead.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: High metabolic cost. Noa: Watch lower back 'sag' during the push-up phase. Boris: Use in AMRAP or EMOM formats for conditioning.

exercises.name: Toes-to-Bar
exercises.setupNotes: Hanging leg raise until toes touch bar.
exercises.committeeNotes: Complete core and grip.

exercises.name: Wall Ball Shot
exercises.setupNotes: Squat with med ball at chest. Explode up and throw ball to a target (9-10ft). Catch and repeat.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: Full body power and endurance. Noa: Keep chest up to avoid ball pulling you forward. Boris: High rep conditioning (20-50 reps).

exercises.name: Kettlebell Thruster
exercises.setupNotes: KBs in rack position. Squat deep. Use hip drive to press KBs overhead in one motion.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: Massive metabolic demand and total body tension. Noa: Don't let KBs pull shoulders into internal rotation. Boris: Use for high-intensity circuits.

exercises.name: Overhead Squat
exercises.setupNotes: Barbell held overhead with a wide snatch grip. Squat deep while keeping bar path over mid-foot.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: The ultimate test of mobility and stability. Noa: Requires excellent thoracic and ankle mobility. Boris: Use for stability and Oly prep.

exercises.name: Arch-Body Hold (Superman)
exercises.setupNotes: Lie face down. Lift chest and thighs off floor simultaneously. Squeeze glutes and lower back.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: Targets the entire posterior chain isometrically. Noa: Avoid neck hyperextension; look at floor. Boris: Essential for postural health.

exercises.name: Chest-to-Bar Pull-Up
exercises.setupNotes: Standard pull-up but pull higher until the chest physically touches the bar. Focus on retraction.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: Increased ROM for greater lat and trap recruitment. Noa: High demand on shoulder internal rotation at the top. Boris: CrossFit staple.

exercises.name: Ring Dip
exercises.setupNotes: Support body on rings. Lower until shoulders are below elbows. Keep rings close to body to avoid flare.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: Instability massively increases pec and tricep recruitment. Noa: High risk for shoulder strain if rings drift. Boris: Elite strength movement.

exercises.name: Front-Rack Reverse Lunge
exercises.setupNotes: Barbell in front rack position. Step back into a lunge. Upright torso required to keep bar from falling.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: High quad and core demand vs back-loaded. Noa: Safer for those with lower back issues. Boris: 8-10 reps per leg.

exercises.name: Box Step-Over
exercises.setupNotes: Hold DBs. Step onto box, then step over to the other side without standing fully at the top. Constant tension.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: Great for glute and quad endurance. Noa: Control the descent to save the Achilles. Boris: Common CrossFit conditioning piece.

exercises.name: American Kettlebell Swing
exercises.setupNotes: Swing KB between legs and drive it all the way overhead until bottom of KB points to ceiling.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: More shoulder ROM than Russian version. Noa: Avoid over-arching the lower back at the top. Boris: High power output required.

exercises.name: Bear Crawl
exercises.setupNotes: Crawl on hands and feet, knees close to floor.
exercises.committeeNotes: Full body coordination and core.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Devil Press
exercises.setupNotes: Burpee onto DBs, jump up, and snatch both DBs overhead in one movement from the floor.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: Absolute metabolic killer. Noa: Spine must stay neutral during the high-velocity pull. Boris: Low rep conditioning (5-10).

exercises.name: Tuck Sit
exercises.setupNotes: Support body with hands, lift knees to chest.
exercises.committeeNotes: Regressed version of L-sit.

exercises.name: Handstand Hold
exercises.setupNotes: Hold handstand against wall or freestanding.
exercises.committeeNotes: Shoulder stability and balance.

exercises.name: Pseudo-Planche Hold
exercises.setupNotes: Lean forward in plank, hands at waist level.
exercises.committeeNotes: Builds straight-arm shoulder strength.

exercises.name: Horse Stance
exercises.setupNotes: Wide stance, thighs parallel to floor.
exercises.committeeNotes: Traditional martial arts leg conditioning.

exercises.name: Duck Walk
exercises.setupNotes: Walk in deep squat position.
exercises.committeeNotes: Hip mobility and leg endurance.

exercises.name: Bird-Dog
exercises.setupNotes: On all fours, extend opposite arm and leg.
exercises.committeeNotes: Core stability and spinal health.

exercises.name: Pallof Press (Iso-Band)
exercises.setupNotes: Hold band at chest, press and hold against resistance.
exercises.committeeNotes: Anti-rotation stability.

exercises.name: Archer Push-up
exercises.setupNotes: Push up with one arm straight to the side.
exercises.committeeNotes: Unilateral push strength.

exercises.name: Typewriter Push-up
exercises.setupNotes: Slide side to side at bottom of push-up.
exercises.committeeNotes: Time under tension and control.

exercises.name: Pseudo-Planche Push-up
exercises.setupNotes: Push up with hands at waist level, leaning forward.
exercises.committeeNotes: Planche progression.

exercises.name: Clap Push-up
exercises.setupNotes: Explosive push up with a clap.
exercises.committeeNotes: Plyometric pushing power.

exercises.name: Single-Arm Push-up
exercises.setupNotes: Push up using only one arm.
exercises.committeeNotes: Elite unilateral strength.

exercises.name: Staggered-Hand Push-up
exercises.setupNotes: One hand higher than the other.
exercises.committeeNotes: Varied pec and shoulder loading.

exercises.name: Tiger Bend Push-up
exercises.setupNotes: Lower to forearms, then push back to hands.
exercises.committeeNotes: Tricep and shoulder focus.

exercises.name: Bench Dip (Feet Elevated)
exercises.setupNotes: Hands on bench, feet on another bench.
exercises.committeeNotes: Increased tricep load.

exercises.name: Incline Push-up
exercises.setupNotes: Hands on elevated surface.
exercises.committeeNotes: Easier variation, lower chest focus.

exercises.name: Hindu Push-up
exercises.setupNotes: Swooping push up movement.
exercises.committeeNotes: Shoulder and spinal mobility.

exercises.name: DB Around the World
exercises.setupNotes: Move DBs in a circle from hips to overhead.
exercises.committeeNotes: Complete shoulder mobility and health.

exercises.name: Around the World (Pull-up Bar)
exercises.setupNotes: Rotate legs in a full circle while hanging.
exercises.committeeNotes: Elite core and oblique strength.

exercises.name: Negative Pull-up
exercises.setupNotes: Lower slowly from the top of a pull-up.
exercises.committeeNotes: Building pull-up strength.

exercises.name: Commando Pull-up
exercises.setupNotes: Side-on grip, pull to alternating shoulders.
exercises.committeeNotes: Bicep and brachialis focus.

exercises.name: Archer Pull-up
exercises.setupNotes: Pull to one side with other arm straight.
exercises.committeeNotes: Unilateral pull strength.

exercises.name: Australian Pull-up (Inverted Row)
exercises.setupNotes: Row with body horizontal under a bar.
exercises.committeeNotes: Foundational back strength.

exercises.name: Face Pull (Resistance Band)
exercises.setupNotes: Pull band toward face, external rotation.
exercises.committeeNotes: Rear delt and rotator cuff.

exercises.name: L-Sit Pull-up
exercises.setupNotes: Pull up while holding an L-sit.
exercises.committeeNotes: Combined pull and core strength.

exercises.name: Explosive Pull-up (Chest-to-Bar)
exercises.setupNotes: Pull up with enough power to touch chest to bar.
exercises.committeeNotes: Power development.

exercises.name: Single-Arm Australian Row
exercises.setupNotes: Single arm horizontal row.
exercises.committeeNotes: Unilateral pull development.

exercises.name: Scapular Pull-up
exercises.setupNotes: Small movement using only scapulae.
exercises.committeeNotes: Shoulder health and pull-up prep.

exercises.name: Shrimp Squat
exercises.setupNotes: Single leg squat with rear leg held.
exercises.committeeNotes: Unilateral quad and balance.

exercises.name: Skater Lunge
exercises.setupNotes: Side-to-side jumping lunge.
exercises.committeeNotes: Lateral power and stability.

exercises.name: Curtsy Lunge
exercises.setupNotes: Lunge with rear leg crossing behind.
exercises.committeeNotes: Glute medius focus.

exercises.name: Sissy Squat (Bodyweight)
exercises.setupNotes: Lean back on balls of feet while squatting.
exercises.committeeNotes: Extreme quad isolation.

exercises.name: Reverse Nordic
exercises.setupNotes: Kneeling, lean torso back.
exercises.committeeNotes: Quad stretch and strength.

exercises.name: Nordic Hamstring Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Kneeling, lower torso slowly.
exercises.committeeNotes: Elite hamstring strength.

exercises.name: Cossack Squat
exercises.setupNotes: Deep side lunge with one leg straight.
exercises.committeeNotes: Hip mobility and strength.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Glute Bridge
exercises.setupNotes: Glute bridge on one leg.
exercises.committeeNotes: Unilateral glute focus.

exercises.name: Hip Thrust (Couch/Bench)
exercises.setupNotes: Back on bench, bar/weight across hips.
exercises.committeeNotes: Maximum glute activation.

exercises.name: Calf Raise (Single-Leg)
exercises.setupNotes: Raise onto toes on one leg.
exercises.committeeNotes: Unilateral calf strength.

exercises.name: Tibialis Raise (Wall)
exercises.setupNotes: Back against wall, lift toes.
exercises.committeeNotes: Anterior shin strength.

exercises.name: Tuck Jump
exercises.setupNotes: Jump and bring knees to chest.
exercises.committeeNotes: Explosive power.

exercises.name: Hollow Rock
exercises.setupNotes: Rock back and forth in hollow body position.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dynamic core stability.

exercises.name: V-Up
exercises.setupNotes: Touch toes and hands in V-shape.
exercises.committeeNotes: Upper and lower ab focus.

exercises.name: Windshield Wiper (Floor)
exercises.setupNotes: Lie on back, rotate legs side to side.
exercises.committeeNotes: Oblique strength.

exercises.name: Windshield Wiper (Bar)
exercises.setupNotes: Hanging rotation of legs.
exercises.committeeNotes: Elite oblique strength.

exercises.name: Knee-to-Elbow (Bar)
exercises.setupNotes: Hanging leg raise until knees touch elbows.
exercises.committeeNotes: Advanced core strength.

exercises.name: Mountain Climber
exercises.setupNotes: Plank position, alternate knees to chest.
exercises.committeeNotes: Core and conditioning.

exercises.name: Spiderman Plank
exercises.setupNotes: Plank position, knee to same-side elbow.
exercises.committeeNotes: Oblique and core.

exercises.name: Plank Shoulder Tap
exercises.setupNotes: Plank position, alternate hand to shoulder.
exercises.committeeNotes: Anti-rotation stability.

exercises.name: Double Under (Jump Rope)
exercises.setupNotes: Jump rope, two turns per jump.
exercises.committeeNotes: Conditioning and coordination.

exercises.name: Single Under
exercises.setupNotes: Standard jump rope.
exercises.committeeNotes: Aerobic conditioning.

exercises.name: Burpee
exercises.setupNotes: Drop to floor, push up, jump.
exercises.committeeNotes: Full body conditioning.

exercises.name: Shadow Boxing
exercises.setupNotes: Controlled punches in the air.
exercises.committeeNotes: Cardio and coordination.

exercises.name: Farmer's Walk (Heavy Bags)
exercises.setupNotes: Carry heavy items for distance.
exercises.committeeNotes: Grip and posture.

exercises.name: Jumping Jacks
exercises.setupNotes: Classic jump with arm/leg spread.
exercises.committeeNotes: Warm-up and cardio.
